title: Astelia samoense
intro: Astelia samoense, also known as the 'Tukuketea', is a species of flowering plant in the Asteliaceae family native to the Pacific Islands, particularly Samoa and Tonga. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing, reduce pain, and fight off infections. The bioactive constituents of Astelia samoense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is traditionally used to make tea, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to treat various health issues in herbal medicine, making it a valuable resource in Pacific Island traditional healing practices.

This classification places Astelia samoense among other monocotyledonous plants, which are characterized by having one seed leaf. It is further classified within the genus Astelia, which is a group of New Zealand native plants. The genus Astelia is divided into several species, with Astelia samoense being one of them.
